scenes.text: Beneath the global averages also lie [emphasis: wide disparities] in service coverage, with SCI in 2023 ranging from 26 in Chad to 92 in Canada. Much of Sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia continue to record lower coverage, while most countries in Europe, North America, and East Asia have achieved high levels of access.

These patterns are closely mirrored by income level. Most high-income countries score above 75–80 on the UHC Service Coverage Index, while many low- and lower-middle-income countries remain in the 50–65 range. Several low-income countries score below 50, highlighting persistent gaps in access to essential health services.
